response:
The error you encounter stems from your WPF project targeting the .NET Framework 4.0 runtime while your main application runs on .NET 7, causing a mismatch in assembly resolution when the system tries to load the legacy presentation framework files.

You must reconfigure your WPF library project to target .NET 7 or .NET 8 instead of .NET Framework to ensure compatibility with your cross-platform main application and eliminate the dependency on the older Windows-specific runtime.

cousin projects in your solution that rely on WPF should be updated to use the modern .NET SDK and ensure that the PackageReference for Microsoft.NET.Sdk.WindowsDesktop is correctly configured to support WPF on the Windows platform while remaining inert on macOS.

After updating the target framework, you will need to verify that your build configuration excludes the WPF assembly from the Mac build process entirely, likely by using conditional compilation symbols or platform-specific project files to prevent the assembly from being loaded where it is unsupported.

This approach allows your application to maintain a single codebase for logic while dynamically loading the appropriate UI framework based on the operating system, resolving the file not found exception without downgrading your main project to .NET Framework.
